content:
Comprehensive Corporate Regulations and Internal Policies (2026)

Human Resources and Workplace Discipline Policy:
The official company policy for the year 2026 establishes standard working hours from Saturday to Wednesday, 09:00 AM to 05:00 PM. To facilitate work-life balance, all employees are entitled to a 30-minute grace period for morning arrivals; arriving up to 09:30 AM will not be penalized as tardiness, provided the time is made up at the end of the day. Attendance on Thursdays is strictly voluntary and will qualify as overtime upon project demands and supervisor approval. Each employee receives 26 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. Leave requests must be logged in the system at least 48 hours in advance. According to the new policy, a maximum of 5 unused leave days can be carried forward to the next year; any remaining balance by the end of December 2026 will automatically expire and will not be encashed. Sick leaves exceeding 3 consecutive days require official validation from an approved medical practitioner and must be submitted to the HR department.

Employee Benefits and Healthcare Guidelines:
The company has renewed its supplementary health insurance contract with 'Saman Insurance' for the current fiscal year. The co-payment (deductible) for hospitalizations, inpatient treatments, and specialized surgeries is set at 10% for employees and their dependents, with the insurance covering the remaining 90%. The maximum annual coverage limit for dental services (including root canals, fillings, and scaling) is 15 million Tomans per employee for the year 2026, subject to a 30% co-payment. Optical expenses, including eyeglasses and contact lenses, are excluded from this contract. Employees must submit their medical bills and invoices to the administrative department by the 10th of each month to ensure reimbursement within the current month's payment cycle.

Software Engineering Protocols and Deployment Pipeline:
All development branches and new features must undergo rigorous Code Review and receive official approval from at least two senior developers before being merged into the 'main' branch. Automated CI/CD test suites executed via GitHub Actions must achieve a 100% pass rate, and test code coverage must not fall below 80%. Direct deployments to production environments without a designated release tag are strictly prohibited. All technical personnel and engineers are required to activate Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) across GitHub, internal corporate networks, and Google workspace accounts. Distributed system and server passwords must be at least 12 complex characters long, incorporating uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special symbols. Passwords must be rotated every 90 days; failure to comply will result in automatic account suspension.

Financial Regulations and Emergency Procurement Directive:
To expedite the project development lifecycle, technical personnel are authorized to make emergency hardware and software purchases up to a threshold of 3 million Tomans without prior alignment or formal approval, provided an official invoice is obtained. Procurement requests exceeding 3 million Tomans will absolutely not be processed or reimbursed without prior written authorization and the signature of the Chief Financial Officer (CFO). All invoices submitted to the accounting department must include a valid national tax ID, an official merchant stamp, and a current date within the year 2026. Hand-written or unstamped receipts will be excluded from the petty cash cycle, and the expenses will be borne by the purchaser. Petty cash settlements are processed every Wednesday.
